scope: V4 experiment-contract freeze, offline regression, release hygiene, and clean source archive

protocol_audit.reason: Phase 0 freezes the contract; Phase 1 must build and lock the 50-case manifest.

coverage: V4 experiment protocol and RunRecord validation

resolved_verification_findings.finding: The V3 architecture document still stated that 60 LLM and 60 Hybrid trials had not run.
resolved_verification_findings.resolution: Replaced the stale boundary with the completed 120/120 live-trial and 423/423 RunRecord result, then updated the V3 packaging hash.

resolved_verification_findings.finding: The first full regression inherited external model credentials and spent substantial time in provider-sensitive paths.
resolved_verification_findings.resolution: Final release regression explicitly removed all model and GitHub credential environment variables before pytest.

resolved_verification_findings.finding: V4 pytest artifacts were not ignored, so a deliberately contaminated hygiene fixture became visible to the repository-level secret scan.
resolved_verification_findings.resolution: Added outputs_v4/ to .gitignore and a V4 regression assertion; the strict secret scan remained unchanged.

claim_boundaries: Phase 0 did not call a live model and does not report a V4 repair rate.
The 50-case repository-disjoint benchmark manifest does not exist yet and must be locked in Phase 1.
No claim is made that Full Agent, LLM Planner, Reflection, Memory, Graph, or Routed Hybrid improves outcomes.
Live V4 data disclosure requires a new explicit user authorization after the 20-case pilot launch gate is ready.
The clean archive proves source reproducibility only; it is not a repair-effectiveness result.

next_gate.objective: Build, reproduce, repository-disjointly split, and hash-lock at least 50 real Python bugs from at least 15 repositories.
